task: I need to send a quick reply to a coworker who gave me a vague update on a test result, and I want to make sure I’m asking for the right follow-up details. Can you draft something brief for that situation?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a brief reply you can send:

“Thanks — can you share the exact test condition, what changed from the last run, and the result in pass/fail terms? If there’s any difference in setup, timing, or sample condition, please include that too.”

If you want, I can make it:
- more casual
- more firm
- or shorter for chat/Slack.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but make it tighter and more technical. I’d want the follow-up to force specifics, not just “what changed.” Something like:

“Can you send the exact test condition, pass/fail result, and any setup differences from the last run? If it’s intermittent, include timing, sample ID, and any logs or photos.”

That’s the kind of answer I can actually use.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a tighter version you can send:

“Can you send the exact test condition, pass/fail result, and any setup differences from the last run? If it’s intermittent, include timing, sample ID, and any logs/photos.”

If you want, I can make it even more blunt for Slack.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, and it actually asks for the details that matter. I’d use that as-is.
